title: High-Performance Computing & Parallel Algorithms

speedup_laws.object: Amdahl's Law and Gustafson's Law: parallel speedup

amdahl.formula: S(N) = 1 / (s + (1-s)/N): EML-2 (rational in N = ln/exp form)
amdahl.bottleneck: Serial fraction s: EML-0 (pure algebraic parameter)

gustafson.formula: S(N) = N - s·(N-1) = s + N(1-s): EML-0 (linear in N!)
gustafson.why: Gustafson: scale problem with N; speedup = linear = EML-0
gustafson.new_finding: GUSTAFSON LAW = EML-0: deepest possible — purely algebraic speedup

semiring.gustafson: EML-0 throughout: problem scales = no transcendental content

analysis.contention: High contention: queue depth grows = EML-2 (queueing theory M/M/1)
analysis.deadlock: Deadlock: EML-∞ (non-constructive: no algorithm detects all)
analysis.shadow_deadlock: shadow(deadlock) = 2: real resource counting = measurement domain

race_condition.why: Race: outcome depends on real timing (measurement); shadow=2

models.butterfly_network: log(N) stages: EML-2 (iterated squaring = EML-1 → log = EML-2)

new_finding.FFT_parallel: Parallel FFT: exp(2πi·k/N) twiddle factors = EML-3
new_finding.why: Complex twiddle factors: parallel FFT is EML-3 algorithm
new_finding.speedup: EML-3 algorithm on EML-3 problem: consistent depth ✓

emergence.object: Emergent behavior in large parallel systems

examples.flash_crowd: Flash crowd: sudden demand spike = TYPE2 Horizon shadow=2
examples.cascade_failure: Cascade failure in distributed system: EML-∞ (cross-type: load(EML-2)⊗topology(EML-0)=∞)
examples.consensus: Byzantine consensus: EML-0 (algebraic majority voting = EML-0 threshold)

verdicts.gustafson: EML-0 THROUGHOUT: deepest law in HPC — purely algebraic
verdicts.parallel_fft: EML-3 (twiddle factors = complex oscillatory): consistent depth
verdicts.new_result: Gustafson's Law=EML-0: parallel computing's most fundamental law is algebraic

key_theorem: HPC-EML Theorem (S337): Gustafson's Law = EML-0: purely algebraic (S(N)=s+N(1-s)). This is the DEEPEST speedup law in HPC — no transcendental content. Amdahl's Law = EML-2 (rational function of N). NEW: Parallel FFT = EML-3 (complex twiddle factors exp(2πi·k/N)): consistent depth — EML-3 algorithm on EML-3 problem. Deadlock/livelock = TYPE2 Horizon with shadow=2 (resource counting = real measurement). Byzantine consensus = EML-0 (algebraic majority voting threshold).
rabbit_hole_log: NEW: Parallel FFT=EML-3 (twiddle factors): consistent with problem depth
Byzantine consensus: EML-0 (majority vote = algebraic threshold)
